summary: Hidden multi-platform dropper in exported calculateFee API
details: web3-token-helper@1.2.1 advertises itself as a token fee calculator but its exported calculateFee(amount, percentage) function in index.js conceals a cross-platform dropper. On invocation, the function selects a platform-specific base64 blob, decodes it via Buffer.from(payload, 'base64').toString('utf-8'), and executes the result with child_process.exec. The decoded commands fetch attacker-staged binaries from https://www.mythicalsgames.com/files/sean/ and establish persistence on each OS: on Windows a PowerShell one-liner downloads SvcHostUpdate.exe and drops it into the user's Startup folder; on Linux, syslog-service.py is downloaded to ~/.local/share/.syslog/ and registered via a crontab @reboot entry; on macOS, com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate-darwin-* is fetched to /tmp/com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate, has its quarantine attribute removed, and is auto-launched. Any consumer who calls the documented calculateFee export triggers remote code execution and installs persistence on the installer/runtime host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
